message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  In 2020, SARS-CoV-2 impacted Georgia, USA. Georgia announced state-wide shelter-in-place on April 2 and partially lifted restrictions on April 27. We analyzed daily incidence of confirmed COVID-19 cases by reporting date, March 2-June 14, in Georgia, Metro Atlanta, and Dougherty County and estimate the time-varying reproduction number,
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  , using R package EpiEstim. The median
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  estimate in Georgia dropped from between 2 and 4 in mid-March, to <2 in late March, and around 1 from mid-April to mid-June. Regarding Metro Atlanta,
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  fluctuated above 1.5 in March and around 1 since April. In Dougherty County, the median
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  declined from around 2 in late March to 0.32 on April 26. In Spring 2020, SARS-CoV-2 transmission in Georgia declined likely because of social distancing measures. However, as restrictions were relaxed in late April, community transmission continued with
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  fluctuating around 1 across Georgia, Metro Atlanta, and Dougherty County as of mid-June.
                </jats:p>
title: Time-varying reproduction numbers of COVID-19 in Georgia, USA, March 2-June 14, 2020
